21.correct: Women performing the Tippani dance traditionally wear a chaniya choli (a decorated skirt and blouse) with ornaments.

wrong.A: A heavy-jewellery saree is not the traditional Tippani costume.
wrong.C: Plain cotton clothing is not the festive attire.
wrong.D: Pants and a shirt are not the traditional costume.

21.fact: The Tippani is a folk dance of women labourers in Gujarat's Saurashtra region.

22.correct: The Deccan Plateau is rich in black soil (regur), formed from the weathering of volcanic basalt.

wrong.A: Alluvial soil is found in the river plains.
wrong.C: Red soil occurs over crystalline rocks, but the Deccan trap is known for black soil.
wrong.D: Laterite soil forms in high-rainfall uplands.

22.fact: Black soil is ideal for cotton cultivation, hence the name 'black cotton soil'.

23.correct: The Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 emphasises a victim-centred and restorative system of justice, rather than purely colonial-style punishment.

wrong.A: It moves away from the colonial penal-code framework.
wrong.B: It is not based on jury trials.
wrong.C: It is not about leniency that is merely accused-oriented.

23.fact: The BNS replaced the Indian Penal Code (IPC) of 1860.

24.correct: In the Mansabdari system, the cavalry/horsemen a mansabdar had to maintain were indicated by the 'sawar' rank.
24.fact: A mansabdar's rank had two numbers: 'zat' (status) and 'sawar' (cavalry to maintain).

25.correct: In April 2025, A. Jayathilak was appointed the 50th Chief Secretary of Kerala.

wrong.B: Sarada Muraleedharan/others are not the appointee cited here.
wrong.C: S. Mahendra Dev is an economist, not the Kerala Chief Secretary.
wrong.D: Sudarshan Venu is a corporate executive.

25.fact: The Chief Secretary is the senior-most civil servant and administrative head of a state.

26.correct: In May 2025, Lieutenant General Pratik Sharma was appointed Commander-in-Chief of the Northern Army Command.

wrong.A: Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth is not the appointee here.
wrong.C: Lt Gen Anindya Sengupta is not the one.
wrong.D: Lt Gen Ram Chandra Tiwari is not the appointee.

26.fact: The Northern Command oversees the sensitive borders in Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh.

27.correct: The 100th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2015 gave effect to the India-Bangladesh Land Boundary Agreement (exchange of enclaves).

wrong.A: The 91st Amendment limited the Council of Ministers' size.
wrong.B: The 97th Amendment dealt with cooperative societies.

27.fact: The 100th Amendment exchanged enclaves and adverse possessions with Bangladesh.

28.correct: The Rajya Sabha has powers equal to the Lok Sabha in the case of Constitutional Amendment Bills.

wrong.A: In Money Bills the Lok Sabha is supreme.
wrong.C: A no-confidence motion can be moved only in the Lok Sabha.
wrong.D: In the President's election both Houses participate, but it is not a 'bill'.

28.fact: A Constitution Amendment Bill must be passed by each House by a special majority.

29.correct: Udhwa Lake is the first Ramsar site of Jharkhand.

wrong.C: Sakkarakottai Bird Sanctuary is in Tamil Nadu.
wrong.D: Therthangal Bird Sanctuary is in Tamil Nadu.

29.fact: Ramsar sites are wetlands of international importance under the Ramsar Convention.

30.correct: In the 1980s, India's indigenous launch vehicle used for satellite launches was the SLV-3.

wrong.C: The ASLV came in the late 1980s as an experimental vehicle, but the first indigenous launcher was the SLV-3.

30.fact: The SLV-3 placed the Rohini satellite in orbit in 1980, with A.P.J. Abdul Kalam as project director.

31.correct: In February 2025, researchers at IIT Guwahati developed a water-repellent, conductive textile.

wrong.A: IIT Delhi was not credited here.
wrong.B: IIT Madras was not the developer cited.
wrong.C: IIT Kharagpur was not the developer.

31.fact: Conductive, water-repellent fabrics are useful for wearable electronics and protective clothing.

32.correct: In Madhya Pradesh, the Majhgawan mine is famous for diamonds.

wrong.B: It is not known for lithium.
wrong.C: It is not known for cobalt.
wrong.D: It is not known for silver.

32.fact: Majhgawan (near Panna) is India's only mechanised diamond mine.

33.correct: The history of the Indian Grand Prix began around 1997, when a Grand Prix was planned to be hosted in Kolkata.
33.fact: The Formula One Indian Grand Prix was actually held at the Buddh International Circuit from 2011 to 2013.

34.correct: Mercosur is a trade bloc associated with South America (its members include Brazil, Argentina, Uruguay and Paraguay).

wrong.A: It is not an Asian bloc.
wrong.B: It is not a European bloc.
wrong.C: It is not an African bloc.

34.fact: Mercosur is a regional common-market grouping in South America.

35.correct: 'Power tumbling' is a term associated with gymnastics.
35.fact: Power tumbling is a gymnastics discipline performed on a sprung track.

36.correct: The Vittala Swamy (Vitthala) Temple is located at Hampi, the former capital of the Vijayanagara Empire.
36.fact: The Vittala Temple at Hampi is famous for its stone chariot and musical pillars.

37.correct: 'Sufiana Kalam', a classical music style using ragas called 'maqam', is associated with Kashmir.

wrong.D: It is not from West Bengal.

37.fact: Sufiana Kalam is the classical music of the Kashmir valley, with Persian and Central Asian influences.

38.correct: Jayaprakash Narayan, popularly known as 'Loknayak', gave the concept of 'Total Revolution' (Sampoorna Kranti).

wrong.A: Morarji Desai did not give this concept.
wrong.B: Rajendra Prasad was India's first President.
wrong.C: Lal Bahadur Shastri gave 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan'.

38.fact: JP led the movement against the Emergency in the mid-1970s.

39.correct: The Annual Survey of India's City-Systems (ASICS) is published by Janaagraha Centre for Citizenship and Democracy.

wrong.B: It is not published by NITI Aayog.
wrong.C: It is not published by the Council for Social Development.
wrong.D: It is not published by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment.

39.fact: ASICS evaluates the quality of urban governance across major Indian cities.

40.correct: The Cayman Islands ranked 79th in the Global Business Complexity Index 2025 (a low complexity ranking).

wrong.B: The Netherlands is not at 79th here.

40.fact: The index ranks jurisdictions by how complex it is to operate a business there.
